burial.plot_notes: Section 14, Lot 1, Albany Rural Cemetery, Menands NY

narrative_blocks.header: He earned a letter of thanks from Abraham
narrative_blocks.body: During the Civil War's Red River Campaign, John Schuyler Crosby carried dispatches through Confederate territory to Admiral Farragut — and President Lincoln personally thanked him for it. He served as assistant adjutant general under General Banks and assistant inspector general under General Philip Sheridan. He was brevetted four times for gallantry, ending the war as brevet lieutenant colonel.

Appointed Governor of Montana Territory by President Chester Arthur in 1882, Crosby organized one of the largest big game hunting expeditions in U.S. history, with a party that included President Arthur, Secretary of War Robert Todd Lincoln, and Senator George Vest. He also played a key role in blocking cattle interests from seizing control of Yellowstone. His grandson Harry Crosby (1898-1929) became one of the central figures of the Lost Generation in Paris, co-founding the Black Sun Press and publishing early works by Hemingway, Joyce, and Hart Crane before his suicide in 1929.

narrative_blocks.header: He saved lives when a yacht capsized
narrative_blocks.body: In July 1876, Crosby was aboard the yacht Mohawk in New York Harbor when a sudden squall overturned it. He made heroic efforts to save lives and the U.S. government later presented him a medal for his conduct. The same year he was appointed U.S. Consul in Florence, Italy — where he helped local authorities capture and prosecute a ring of forgers.

cross_connections.display_label: Both at the 1883 presidential big game hunt in Montana Territory — Crosby organized it, Lincoln attended as Secretary of War.

parents.father_research_notes: John Schuyler Crosby, 5th Governor of Montana Territory
parents.mother_research_notes: Harriet Van Rensselaer Crosby (1838-1911); youngest dau. of Gen. Stephen Van Rensselaer IV (last patroon of Rensselaerwyck); granddaughter of Gen. Philip Schuyler. Not a separate entry.

narrative_blocks.body: Angelica Schuyler Crosby married diplomat John Brooks Henderson Jr. in 1903 in Jefferson County, West Virginia. Unable to have children, they adopted Beatrice Van Rensselaer Henderson. Angelica died in 1907 at approximately 35, leaving Henderson a widower with an infant daughter.

narrative_blocks.header: His grandmother discovered the greenhouse effect
narrative_blocks.body: John Brooks Henderson Jr. was the grandson of Eunice Newton Foote, who published the first greenhouse effect paper in 1856. He graduated Harvard 1891, served as private secretary to diplomat John W. Foster, traveled with General Nelson Miles to Europe and the Ottoman Empire, and became a Smithsonian Institution Board of Regents member in 1911.

After John Jr. and his wife Angelica took in an adopted daughter, Beatrice, a bitter rift followed: in 1931 Beatrice tried to have her grandmother Mary Foote Henderson declared incompetent. Mary retaliated by publicly revealing that Beatrice was adopted and cutting her from the will in favor of her secretary.

narrative_blocks.header: A diplomat who collected Caribbean shells
narrative_blocks.body: John Henderson Jr. turned a boyhood hobby into expertise, mounting expeditions to the West Indies to study marine shell life and donating his collection to the U.S. National Museum. A lizard, Anolis hendersoni, was named in his honor.

career.role: Private Secretary to diplomat John W. Foster

parents.father_research_notes: Adopted daughter of John Brooks Henderson Jr. (HD3150). Biological parents unknown.
parents.mother_research_notes: Adoptive mother X02109 died 1907 — before or around time of Beatrice's birth.

sources.short: Wikipedia — John Henderson Jr.; Mary Foote Henderson

narrative_blocks.body: Beatrice Van Rensselaer Henderson was adopted by John Brooks Henderson Jr. and raised partly by her formidable grandmother, Mary Foote Henderson. Though not a blood descendant, she carried the family name, married diplomat Joseph Wholean in 1926, and shared fully in the dynasty’s tangled fortunes.

burialCemetery.name: Albany Rural Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Major rural cemetery north of Albany NY; final resting place of President Chester A. Arthur and many notable New Yorkers. Talcott burials include T01818 Bvt. Lt. Col. George Henry Talcott (USMA 1831, Mexican War, wounded Molino del Rey), I00282 Catharine Jane Starke Talcott (his wife), T01819 Brig. Gen. Sebastian Visscher Talcott (compiler of the Talcott Genealogy book), and I00281 Olivia Maria Shearman Talcott (his wife).

crossConnections.display_label: Both at the 1883 presidential big game hunt in Montana Territory — Crosby organized it, Lincoln attended as Secretary of War.
